name: Triple Chocolate Cheesecake
recipeIngredient: 1 1/4 cups chocolate wafer cookies, crushed fine
4 ounces milk chocolate, melted and cooled
4 ounces white chocolate, melted and cooled
4 ounces bitter chocolate or semisweet chocolate, cooled
4 ounces bitter chocolate or semisweet chocolate, -
recipeInstructions:
GARNISH
sliced almonds -- toasted
Crust: Preheat oven to 350°F. In a bowl, mix melted butter and crumbs. Press over the bottom of a 10-in. springform pan. Bake for 8-10 minutes until set. Cool on a rack. With unmelted butter, grease the pan sides. Wrap the outside of the pan with heavy-duty foil; refrigerate. Reduce oven to 325°F.
Filling: In a large bowl with an electric mixer on medium-high speed, beat cream cheese for 2-3 minutes until fluffy. Gradually beat in sugar until smooth. With the mixer on medium speed, beat in eggs, one at a time, just until incorporated. Beat in cream. Divide batter evenly among three medium bowls (about 2 1/2 cups each). Stir melted milk chocolate into one bowl, white chocolate into the next, and bitter chocolate or semisweet chocolate into the last. Pour milk chocolate batter into the prepared crust in an even layer. Pour and spread white chocolate batter over milk chocolate layer; do not let batters run together. Top with bitter chocolate or semisweet chocolate layer, carefully spreading evenly. Set springform pan in the center of a large roasting pan: place on oven rack. Pour boiling water into the roasting pan to reach halfway up the springform pan. Bake for 2 hours or until set. Turn off the oven; let stand in the oven for 1 hour. Transfer springform pan to the rack; let cool completely. Remove foil. Cover; refrigerate at least 8 hours or overnight. Glaze: In a saucepan, heat cream to a gentle boil. Place chocolate in a bowl, add cream. Let stand 1 minute; whisk until smooth. Pour over the top of cheesecake; spread to edges. Chill for 15 minutes or until set. Run a thin-bladed knife around the edge of the pan; carefully remove the pan side. Transfer cake to a serving plate. Let stand at room temperature for 1/2 hour. Garnish top edge of cake with toasted sliced almonds, if desired. Slice cake with a large knife; between slices. Wipe blade clean and dip blade in warm water.
